definitions: Products that are sold quickly and at a relatively low cost. Examples include non-durable household goods such as packaged foods, beverages, toiletries, candies, and other consumables.
Goods that have a short shelf life, either as a result of high consumer demand or because they are perishable.
examples: The company specializes in the distribution of Fast-Moving Consumer Goods.
Supermarkets rely heavily on the sale of Fast-Moving Consumer Goods.
Marketing strategies for Fast-Moving Consumer Goods often focus on brand recognition and competitive pricing.
